pages.text: The $(9)Fortron Capacitor$() serves as an energy storage and management device for MFFS machines. It's designed to collect Fortron generated by $(l:machines/coercion_deriver)Coercion Deriver$()s and transfer it to other machines that need $(l:getting_started/fortron)Fortron$() to operate, essentially acting as a "hub".

pages.title: Usage
pages.text: Place the Capacitor within range (default 15 blocks) of your $(l:machines/coercion_deriver)Coercion Deriver$() and set its frequency, this can be changed either by:$(li)Right-clicking the Capacitor and entering the desired frequency number into its GUI$(li)Shift-right-clicking the Capacitor while holding a $(l:tools/frequency_card)Frequency Card$().

Activate the Fortron Capacitor by supplying it with a Redstone signal or toggling the redstone button in the top-left corner of its GUI. Once activated, the Capacitor will begin transferring $(l:getting_started/fortron)Fortron$() between other MFFS devices that use the same frequency and are within range (default 15 blocks).

pages.title: Upgradability
pages.text: A Fortron Capacitor may be augmented with various modules to enhance its capabilities:$(li)$(l:upgrade_modules/scale_module)$(5)Scale Module$(): Enables the Capacitor to link with other MFFS machines over a larger distance. Each module increases the range by 1 block from the default of 15 blocks.$(li)$(l:upgrade_modules/speed_module)$(5)Speed Module$(): Allows the Capacitor to transfer $(l:getting_started/fortron)Fortron$() to and from machines more quickly.
$(li)$(l:upgrade_modules/capacity_module)$(5)Capacity Module$(): Increases the amount of $(l:getting_started/fortron)Fortron$() the Capacitor is able to store.
